segments.text: Hi, everyone, and thank you for joining us at Autodesk Defcon 2026 here in the Netherlands.
Today we will be sharing how we are leveraging the power of AI directly inside Revit to transform the QA processes.
This is through an AI-driven QA chatbot.
The focus of this session is simple.
How AI can help design teams move faster, reduce manual effort, and improve confidence in submission-ready models and drawings.
My name is Pujar Astogi and I am the Divasional General Manager and I am joined with my colleague, Kuber, who is a principal digital engineer from Global Digital Ventures.
That's the name of our division in Montfag Donald.
Together we work closely with our design teams to identify where digital innovation and in particular AI can create meaningful scalable value in our day-to-day delivery.
So let's start with the question.
Do these challenges sound familiar?
Design teams often struggle to complete QA on time.
There's a lack of confidence that drawings are truly submission-ready.
QA tends to happen very late in the program, often just before delivery.
Most importantly, teams spend more time finding issues than fixing them.
And much of its this effort relies heavily on manual checks.
These challenges are due to lack of expertise.
They are result of time pressure, increasing model complexity, and traditional document-based QA workflows.
So let's compare a traditional QA process with an AI-assisted one.
In a manual workflow, teams typically fix the model, print PDFs, manual checks, drawings, go through SME reviews, and then loop back again to fix errors.
With AI assistance, the flow changes.
Designers select relevant QA checks, receive instant reports directly from the model, and fix issues earlier before they propagate.
The result is clear.
Less reputative checking, faster turnaround, and SME effort focused on higher value decision rather than routine validation.
So with AI-assisted QA, the agents will trigger the logical automations within Revit, developed as a separate function.
That cater specialist takes.
See how it works in the next slide.
Thanks for watching.
Thanks for watching.
Thanks for watching.
Thanks for watching.
So what did this enable?
AI-assisted QA significantly reduces manual checking effort.
It delivers a measurable time saving and frees up the specialist reviewers.
And most importantly, it is shifting QA from a reactive document-based activity to a proactive model-based validation process.
The outcome is a better design quality, faster delivery, and a QA approach that can scale consistently across projects and disciplines.
To close, we believe the future of QA in AEC lies in combining human expertise with AI acceleration.
When AI handles the deputative and time-consuming checks, engineers and designers can focus on what they do best, which is judgment, creativity, and problem solving.
Thank you for your time, and we hope this session has given you a practical view of how AI can reimagine QA in Revit.
